Free PDF guide

From chatbot prompts to automated AI workflows in higher education.

A clear four-level model for AI use in HE: from ad-hoc chatbots to repeatable workflows your teams can follow—without drowning in hype or one-off prompts.

  • Free 10-page PDF
  • 5-min read
  • Built for higher education
  • The 4-level model

No spam. · Instant download.

Get your free copy

Instant

Enter your name and work email. We'll send your copy straight away.

We will use your details to send this resource and relevant WorkSmart-AI follow-up. Read our privacy policy.

Inside the guide

The 4-level AI Use Hierarchy

A simple progression that turns ad hoc chatbot use into repeatable systems your staff can rely on. Most teams stay stuck on Level 1 - and miss the bigger gains.

  1. 01

    Level 1 - Conversational

    Open a chatbot, paste, prompt, refine.

    Where most staff start - and where most AI training stops.

  2. 02

    Level 2 - Structured

    Reusable prompts, templates and instructions.

    Same task every week? Stop typing it from scratch.

  3. 03

    Level 3 - Configured

    Custom GPTs and Copilot agents tuned to your role.

    Your guidelines, tone of voice and source files - on tap.

  4. 04

    Level 4 - Agentic

    Workflows that run with minimal manual effort.

    Triggered by a meeting, an inbox, a calendar - not by you.

See the worked example in the guide — get your free copy →

What's inside

Build an AI progression your staff can actually follow

A task-every-term approach: turning meeting notes into a clean action summary. You'll see how that task evolves from a basic chatbot prompt to an automated workflow, using tools many institutions already have.

  • Why most AI training stops at 20% of what's possible.
  • The four-level AI Use Hierarchy every HE team should know.
  • One familiar task, traced from chatbot prompt to automated workflow.
  • Where Copilot, Teams and Power Automate fit in the progression.
  • How to move staff from ad hoc prompts to repeatable systems.
  • Time-saving estimates at each level of the hierarchy.
Get the free guide

Who it's for

Written for everyone making AI work in HE

Whether you're shaping policy or saving time on your inbox, the same four-level model applies.

Senior leaders & L&D

Frame your AI strategy in language your staff and trustees can act on - not just hype.

Academic teams

Move from one-off prompts to repeatable AI for teaching prep, feedback and research admin.

Professional services

See where the real time savings live in admissions, comms, finance, HR and student support.

2–3 hours

saved per staff member, per week

“Moving from Level 1 to Level 2 alone can save 2–3 hours per staff member per week across common tasks. For a 3,000-person institution, that is the equivalent of nearly 180 full-time roles' worth of time, every year.”

From the WorkSmart-AI Higher Education Guide

Quick answers

Before you download

How long is the guide?

10 pages, designed to read in around five minutes. One worked example, four levels, and the time-saving you can expect at each.

Do I need any special tools to use it?

No. The guide uses tools many UK universities already have, including Microsoft Copilot, Teams and Power Automate. The principles work with any modern AI assistant.

Will I get spammed?

No. We'll email the guide and a small number of relevant follow-ups. You can unsubscribe in one click at any time.

Can I share it with my team?

Yes - please do. The guide is written to be circulated. If you'd like a session walking your team through it, just reply to the email.

Ready to take your team beyond the guide?

WorkSmart-AI delivers structured AI training built specifically for higher education - from foundational literacy to agentic workflows.

Book a free call Just send me the guide
